title: Steak, Watermelon & Peanut Salad
summary: A true summer banger. Zingy, fresh, a bit salty, sweet and sour with some chilli heat. This sally is a belter, you’re gonna love it.

ingredients.label: Large Handful of Roasted Salted Peanuts
ingredients.name: Salted Roasted Peanut

ingredients.label: 2 x 400g Packs of Prepared Watermelon Chunks
ingredients.name: Watermelon

method.description:
Get a large frying pan searing hot – this will take a couple of
minutes over a super high heat. Meanwhile, cut the limes in half.
Thinly slice the red chilli and roughly chop the peanuts. Scrape
the chilli and peanuts into a small mixing bowl and set aside.

Season the steaks liberally with black pepper on both sides.
Drizzle some oil into the frying pan and, once sizzling, add the
steaks. Fry for 2 mins on each side.

Dressing time. Crush the unpeeled garlic clove into a large mixing
bowl. Add the fish sauce, soy sauce, light brown sugar and a
splash of neutral oil. Squeeze in the juice from the halves of one
lime. Whisk together with a fork.

Once the steaks are cooked, transfer them to the chopping board
and leave to rest while you assemble the salad.

Separate the baby gem lettuces into leaves straight into the bowl
of salad dressing. Toss together, then tip out onto a large platter.
Scatter over the watermelon chunks.

Thinly slice the steaks, then lay the slices over the salad. Squeeze
over the juice of the remaining lime halves. Scatter over the
chopped chilli and peanuts to finish. Serve at the table and let
people help themselves.

chefs.name: Sophie Godwin
chefs.summary: Sophie Godwin is a cookbook author, food writer and chef. When she’s not catering weddings, she is rustling up accessible cookable bangers. She's helped us write multiple Mob cookbooks over the years and her recipes never disappoint. Her debut cookbook Sundays is out now.
